title: rebuild labels with build --label
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

If the image has been built with dynamic --labels, the next build with other dynamic labels does not neforce rebuild of labels layer

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
$ podman -v; set -x; podman image rm podman_issue; podman build -t podman_issue . --label "dynamic_label1=value1"; echo ""; podman inspect podman_issue --format "LABEL {{ .Config.Labels.dynamic_label1 }};{{ .Labels.dynamic_label1 }}"; podman build -t podman_issue . --label "dynamic_label1=value2"; echo ""; podman inspect podman_issue --format "LABEL {{ .Config.Labels.dynamic_label1 }};{{ .Labels.dynamic_label1 }}"; podman image rm podman_issue; podman build -t podman_issue . --label "dynamic_label1=value2"; echo ""; podman inspect podman_issue --format "LABEL {{ .Config.Labels.dynamic_label1 }};{{ .Labels.dynamic_label1 }}"; set +x
```
```
podman version 3.1.2
+ podman image rm podman_issue
Untagged: localhost/podman_issue:latest
Deleted: 4391583b1b31d53ed3f64c86dfb29cbdb7635856431034e30e3bd99d111c7cca
+ podman build -t podman_issue . --label dynamic_label1=value1
STEP 1: FROM docker.io/adoptopenjdk/openjdk11:alpine-jre
STEP 2: CMD ls -la .
STEP 3: COMMIT podman_issue
--> 504c4f91411
504c4f914111e239a0465855d9ef418d692e3a1e327d8d24e1278995ced17259
+ echo ''

+ podman inspect podman_issue --format 'LABEL {{ .Config.Labels.dynamic_label1 }};{{ .Labels.dynamic_label1 }}'
LABEL value1;value1
+ podman build -t podman_issue . --label dynamic_label1=value2
STEP 1: FROM docker.io/adoptopenjdk/openjdk11:alpine-jre
STEP 2: CMD ls -la .
--> Using cache 504c4f914111e239a0465855d9ef418d692e3a1e327d8d24e1278995ced17259
STEP 3: COMMIT podman_issue
--> 504c4f91411

LABEL value1;value1
+ podman image rm podman_issue
Untagged: localhost/podman_issue:latest
Deleted: 504c4f914111e239a0465855d9ef418d692e3a1e327d8d24e1278995ced17259
+ podman build -t podman_issue . --label dynamic_label1=value2
STEP 1: FROM docker.io/adoptopenjdk/openjdk11:alpine-jre
STEP 2: CMD ls -la .
STEP 3: COMMIT podman_issue
--> e36b8938983
e36b89389833bbd5eb7578fde3cc5c549684230ab7fc5614bcb1a54eaeb3649a
+ echo ''

+ podman inspect podman_issue --format 'LABEL {{ .Config.Labels.dynamic_label1 }};{{ .Labels.dynamic_label1 }}'
LABEL value2;value2
+ set +x

```


**Describe the results you received:**

The label value is set only if image is rebuild becaus of lower layers are missing/changed

**Describe the results you expected:**

The dynamic labels on command line for build should enforce label layer recreation.


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

docker works as expected

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 3.1.2
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

comments.body:
Are you saying the second build with the label is replacing all labels, as opposed to appending on to the first one?  Is this what Docker does?  I am not sure this is a great default, since then there would be no way to remove a label?

@nalind @vrothberg @TomSweeneyRedHat WDYT?
The second build will remove/override the dynamic labels defined in the first build using Docker.

Additionally, if first build had no label, a second build with `--label` _will not add it_!
Let's use a Dockerfile with a RUN command to show caching more convincingly:
```
FROM docker.io/adoptopenjdk/openjdk11:alpine-jre
RUN date > date.txt
CMD cat date.txt
```

```
function inspect { echo; podman inspect podman_issue --format "LABEL {{ .Config.Labels.dynamic_label1 }};{{ .Labels.dynamic_label1 }}"; echo; }; 
podman -v; set -x; podman image rm podman_issue; podman build -t podman_issue .; inspect; podman build -t podman_issue . --label "dynamic_label1=value2"; inspect; podman image rm podman_issue; podman build -t podman_issue . --label "dynamic_label1=value2"; inspect; set +x
```

<details>


So the point is not whether 2nd build is replacing vs appending labels.  It's that AFAICT when the first build is cached, 2nd build `--label` is simply ignored!  :boom: 
`podman rm` workaround drops the cached layers and then the label was applied, but it also meant the RUN command was re-executed :-1:
I also tried `--no-cache`, works too (but had more confusing effect on following build).

:pray: My expectation was that `--label` flag is functionally equivallent to appending an an explicit LABEL command to the Dockerfile.

That does work well, build steps are cached (including RUN) and label (or absence of) is applied independent of previous run:
```
podman -v; set -x; podman image rm podman_issue; podman build -t podman_issue .; inspect; cp Dockerfile Dockerfile.value1; echo 'LABEL dynamic_label1=value1' >> Dockerfile.value1; podman build -t podman_issue -f Dockerfile.value1; inspect; cp Dockerfile Dockerfile.value2; echo 'LABEL dynamic_label1=value2' >> Dockerfile.value2; podman build -t podman_issue -f Dockerfile.value2; inspect; podman build -t podman_issue .; inspect; set +x
```

I'm also using 3.1.2, Fedora 33.  My podman info is essentially same, except I have:
  cgroupManager: cgroupfs
  cgroupVersion: v1
    Backing Filesystem: extfs

The wrong reuse also works the other way around: if previous build applied a `--label`, next build without the flag still reuses the cached image with the label:
```
podman -v; set -x; podman image rm podman_issue; podman build -t podman_issue . --label "dynamic_label1=value2"; inspect; podman build -t podman_issue .; inspect; set +x
```

<details>

A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

One problem with above patch (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/3370#issuecomment-918024546) is we are always commiting even if same command is provided twice example `podman build --label "hello=world" -t image .` would re-commit even if we are going to use exact same command again. Therefore i am favor of tracking history of label changes and have it just like docker.

I went ahead with how docker is behaving for this case as that seemed more elegant to me but we can discuss on that.

@cben @dosmanak you can try following commit https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/3517 with buildah as `buildah build --layers --label "hello=world" -t podman_issue .` is equivalent to `podman build -t podman_issue . --label "hello=world"`. Once accepted we can vendor this into podman.
